title: Determine the exact value of R(K7, K4-e)
micro_paper_assessment: Exact closure remains open. The current packet isolates a theorem-facing local structure slice, but it is still too thin for the micro-paper lane without the exact 28 versus 29 endpoint.
family_affinity: strong local affinity to the R(K_t, K4-e) almost-clique family via the neighborhood-matching and anti-neighborhood recursion template
generalization_signal: moderate: for K4-e-free graphs with alpha <= r, the same proof gives matching-plus-isolates neighborhoods, Delta <= 2r, and anti-neighborhood independence at most r-1
candidate_theorem_slice: Any K4-e-free graph G with alpha(G) <= 6 has Delta(G) <= 12; each degree-12 neighborhood is 6K2, each degree-11 neighborhood is 5K2 + K1, and every anti-neighborhood has independence number at most 5.
next_action: If this lane is continued, focus on the degree-11/12 local template and the resulting alpha <= 5 anti-neighborhood subproblem; do not escalate to broad search without first confirming that this structural slice is not already standard.
